column4: abase < OF abaissier, regular ME verbal stem + -ing
substantivized plural of Latinate adjective is an unusual formation, no independent secular ground
Latin acceptatio > OF acceptation, regular -ation loanword stream
Latin adjurare, legal/biblical Latinate loan, regular
adulterer (Latin adulter) + native -ess, regular agent-fem. suffix pattern (murderess, sorceress)
Latin adversitas > OF adversite, regular loan
Latin affirmare > OF affermer, regular loan
OF agate, attested ME loanword (gem name)
Gk agonia > Lat/OF agonie, attested ME (Wycliffe Luke 22:44)
Hebrew transliteration (biblical wood-name), source-language transcription not an English coinage
a- + loof (nautical Du/Fr loef), Tudor-era loan formation, regular a- prefixing pattern (adrift, ashore)
amaze (OE amasian) + -ment, regular abstract-noun suffix on long-attested stem
OF embusche/enbusshen, attested ME verb, regular respelling
AN amercier, attested ME legal term
angel (OE/OF loan) attested ME + regular genitive
Gk anison > OF anis, attested ME (Chaucer)
OF enoint(i)er, attested ME anointen + regular -edst
ME naprons w/ metanalysis to apron, attested 15th c
armour (OF) + bearer, transparent native/French compound, common Bible-translation pattern
OF essaier, attested ME assaien; cf. KJ's own Essayes (1585) title stem
transparent native coinage at + one + -ment, self-evident regular formation independent of any single corpus
OE æl "awl" with medial w dropped — letter-drop corruption signature, cf. couch/crouch
Gk/Lat austerus > OF austere, attested ME
OF avengier/vengier, attested ME, regular agent noun
Gk baptisma > ME baptym, attested; regular plural
OE beorcan (dog) or native "bark" (tree rind, ON borkr), regular -ed
OF bacin, attested ME, common EME spelling-variant of basin
native "bakke/backe" replaced by "bat" 16th c, regular semantic continuity + plural
OF bataille, attested ME (Chaucer), regular plural
Gk/Lat transliteration of Hebrew bedolach, source-language transcription
beauty (OF) + -fy, regular Latinate-pattern verb (cf. purify, glorify)
analogical irregular plural of beef (OF buef), attested EME pattern parallel to loaves/calves
OE gelyfan > believe + -er, regular agent noun
ON bylgja, attested EME nautical loan, regular
OF butor altered, attested ME bittour/EME bittern, regular
blessed + -ness, regular double-suffix pattern (cf. righteousness, wickedness)
blood + guilty + -ness, transparent native compound from long-attested stems
OE/OF bolt + -ed, milling-sieve sense attested 15-16th c
bond + maid, native compound (cf. bondman, attested ME)
bond + servant, transparent native/OF compound (cf. bondman)
ON buð/OE both, attested ME bothe
ME boi, attested (Chaucer), origin obscure but well attested
OE broþru variant, attested ME britheren + genitive
broken + hearted, transparent native compound, well-attested literary pattern
OF bruit (report/rumor), attested EME sense independent of biblical use
OE bula + -ock dim., attested ME + genitive
MDu bolwerc, attested EME military loan
busy + body, transparent native compound, regular EME coinage pattern
by + way, native OE compound, attested ME biweyes
Lat campus, attested EME military loan
candle + stick, native/Lat-OE compound, attested ME candelstikke
